summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates hostname to oastify.com on require()
details: On module load, index.js reads os.hostname() and issues an HTTP GET to a Burp Collaborator subdomain of the form `shared-components.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com/shared-components`, leaking the installer's hostname to an attacker-controlled out-of-band collaborator endpoint. The package is published under `@onum-releases` and self-describes in package.json as a dependency-confusion / scope-takeover demonstration targeting an internal scope, which it does not own. Any developer or CI machine that require()s this package will silently transmit its hostname to the collaborator domain. The 'authorized research / benign PoC' framing in the description is not enforceable on installers and the network beacon executes unconditionally on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
